title: Epitaph [1] On A Robin Redbreast.
content:
Tread lightly here, for here, 'tis said,
    When piping winds are hush'd around,
    A small note wakes from underground,
    Where now his tiny bones are laid.
    No more in lone and leafless groves,
    With ruffled wing and faded breast,
    His friendless, homeless spirit roves;
    --Gone to the world where birds are blest!
    Where never cat glides o'er the green,
    Or school-boy's giant form is seen;
    But Love, and Joy, and smiling Spring
    Inspire their little souls to sing!
meaning:
This evocative piece by Samuel Rogers, titled "Epitaph [1] On A Robin Redbreast.", represents a masterful exploration of classic. The lines capture a profound emotional resonance...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
